# AmbiSecure — extended LLM-friendly site index

> Full index of every indexable page on https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/.
> Generated by `tools/build-llms-full.py` from the search index. Companion to [llms.txt](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/llms.txt).

Each entry is `[title](url) — one-line summary`. Summaries come from the page `<meta name="description">`. No PII is exposed; the file is regenerated whenever the site rebuilds.

## Products

- [AmbiSEC IoT Security Co-Processor — dual-domain SE](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/iot-security-coprocessor/) — AmbiSEC is a dual-domain IoT security co-processor: MCU runs the app, the crypto domain holds keys, signs, verifies, and authenticates secure OTA.
- [AmbiSecure BioKey — Biometric USB Security Key](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/biokey/) — Biometric USB security key with on-device fingerprint sensor. FIDO2 + match-on-device. AAL3-eligible. USB-A or USB-C; NFC variant.
- [AmbiSecure eSIM — SGP.22 / SGP.32 eUICC + OIDC](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/esim-solution/) — Ambimat eSIM platform: SGP.22 consumer, SGP.32 M2M, automotive eUICC, with optional OpenID Connect applet for end-user identity verification.
- [AmbiSecure IoT Security Applets — Secure-Element Applets](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/iot-security-applets/) — JavaCard applets for IoT device identity. Provisioning, attestation, signed firmware update, mutual TLS credential storage. CC EAL6+ secure element.
- [AmbiSecure IoT Solution — end-to-end platform](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/iot-solution/) — End-to-end IoT security: secure-element chipset, key manager service, FIDO-enabled enterprise MFA, attestation pipeline. OEM-friendly.
- [AmbiSecure OnePass Card — FIDO2 smart card](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-card/) — The AmbiSecure OnePass Card is a FIDO2 smart card with resident credentials, NFC + USB, brandable form factor — employee ID and authenticator in one card.
- [AmbiSecure PIV Bio Card — PIV with on-card fingerprint match](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/piv-bio-card/) — PIV-compatible JavaCard applet on a biometric smart card with on-card fingerprint sensor. Three-factor auth (have / know / are) on one ID-1 card. CC EAL6+ SE.
- [AmbiSecure PIV USB Key — PIV applet in a USB-A / USB-C key](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/piv-usb-key/) — PIV-compatible JavaCard applet in a USB-A / USB-C key. CCID + WebAuthn dual interface, smart-card logon, PKCS#11, SSH agent forwarding, CC EAL6+ SE.
- [AmbiSecure PKCS Signature Suite — Token + Middleware](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/pkcs-signature-suite/) — Hardware signature token with PKCS#11 / PKCS#15 middleware. Cross-platform integration: Acrobat, Office, Mozilla, OpenSC, Windows BaseCSP.
- [AmbiSecure Secure Mail Suite — Email + Document Signing](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/secure-mail-suite/) — White-label platform for S/MIME email signing, email encryption, PDF/PKCS#7 document signing. Hardware-backed via smart card, PIV, or PKCS#11 token.
- [AmbiSecure Tappable — NFC FIDO2 Authenticator](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/tappable/) — NFC-only FIDO2 authenticator in card, keyfob, and sticker form factors. Mobile-first authentication and contactless physical access.
- [BioKey On-Device TOTP — Hardware-Sealed OTP](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/biokey-on-device-totp/) — OATH TOTP and HOTP for AmbiSecure BioKey: OTP seeds stay sealed in a secure element, every code released only after fingerprint verification. FIDO2 stays primary.
- [Digital Signature Token — hardware PKI token](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/digital-signature-token/) — Hardware PKI signature token. On-token RSA/ECC, PKCS#11, X.509. eIDAS-compatible flows. Pilot batches from 50 units.
- [FIDO2 Nano-Card Applet — embedded SE authenticator](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/fido2-nano-sim-applet/) — FIDO2 / CTAP2 applet on a CC EAL6+ secure element. Nano-card (4FF) and MFF2 packages. ECC P-256, ISO/IEC 7816 contact + 14443 contactless.
- [JavaCard Applets — FIDO, PIV, OpenPGP, NDEF](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/javacard-applets/) — JavaCard applet platform: FIDO, PIV, OpenPGP, Door Access, Certificate Storage, NDEF, OpenID Connect, SCP02, biometric variants, IoT Security. Multi-applet on one chip.
- [OnePass Bio Card — biometric FIDO2 smart card](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-bio-card/) — Biometric FIDO2 smart card with on-card fingerprint match. Template never leaves silicon. No PIN, no reader, no shared secret. Phishing-resistant.
- [OnePass Platform — AmbiSecure | Hardware-backed identity, end to end](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-platform/) — OnePass is a unified hardware-backed identity platform — smart card, USB key, validation server, lifecycle. Built for device-bound rather than syncable identity.
- [OnePass USB Key — FIDO2 hardware security key](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-usb-key/) — The AmbiSecure OnePass USB Key is a FIDO2 hardware security key — USB-A or USB-C, no battery, optional PIV applet. Phishing-resistant enterprise authentication.
- [PIV Nano-Card Applet — secure-element PIV](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/piv-nano-sim-applet/) — PIV-compatible JavaCard applet on a CC EAL6+ secure element. Nano-card (4FF) and MFF2 packages. Four cert slots, RSA + ECC, hardware-backed store.
- [PIV Smart Card — contact + contactless ID-1](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/piv-card/) — PIV-compatible JavaCard applet on an ID-1 smart card with contact + contactless. Four cert slots, RSA + ECC, Windows/macOS logon, PKCS#11, FIPS 201 surface.
- [Products — FIDO, JavaCard & IoT SE](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/) — AmbiSecure product catalogue: FIDO2 OnePass smart cards, biometric cards, USB keys, NFC tappable, IoT security co-processor, JavaCard applets, digital signature tokens.

## Services

- [AmbiSecure ePassport Platform Engineering — Backend + PKI](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/epassport-platform/) — End-to-end ePassport platform engineering. Backend, frontend, PKI (CSCA/DSC/PKD), enrolment, personalisation. ICAO 9303-aligned design.
- [AmbiSecure FIDO Validation Server — Multi-tenant FIDO2 SaaS](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/fido-validation-server/) — FIDO-based passwordless authentication platform. Multi-tenant SaaS. JS API + REST. WebAuthn, CTAP2, MDS lookup. Per-tenant policy and attestation verification.
- [Bio Enrollment App — match-on-card biometrics](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/bio-enrollment-app/) — Companion app for AmbiSecure biometric authenticators. Match-on-card / match-on-device enrollment. ISO 19794-2 templates, multi-finger, audit-ready.
- [FIDO Validation Server demo — access posture](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/fido-validation-server/demo/) — FIDO Validation Server demo access is request-based — no public tenant signup, no open SaaS preview. Schedule a guided evaluation with engineering.
- [JavaCard Applet Development — full-cycle JCOP](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/javacard-development/) — Full-cycle JavaCard / JCOP applet development. JavaCard 3.0, GlobalPlatform 2.3, JCOP 3.1, PIV, FIDO U2F, FIDO2 CTAP, WebAuthn. CAP files + source delivered.
- [Multi-Card Applet Loader — SCP03 CAP loader](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/multi-card-applet-loader/) — Factory tool that loads CAP files onto a stack of smart cards over GlobalPlatform 2.3.1 SCP03. Multi-reader, HSM-backed issuer keys, audit log.
- [NDEF Personalisation Tool — NFC Forum NDEF](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/ndef-personalisation/) — Programs NDEF URL, vCard, or custom records onto AmbiSecure card and token surfaces. NFC Forum Type 4, bulk programming, audit log.
- [Security Key Manager — enterprise key lifecycle](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/security-key-manager/) — Administrator-facing tool for OnePass USB Key inventory, lifecycle, and audit. Set/Get Master Key, Generate Key Pair, Sign data, audit log, REST API.
- [Services — AmbiSecure | JavaCard, FIDO server, personalisation tooling](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/) — AmbiSecure engineering services — JavaCard applet development, FIDO Validation Server, tool-chain development (bio enrollment, key manager, loader, NDEF).
- [Tool-Chain Development — bio, key manager, loader](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/) — Smart-card and FIDO tool-chain development: bio enrollment, security key manager, multi-card applet loader, NDEF personalisation. SCP03 loading, factory MES integration.

## Solutions

- [Closed-loop ticketing — transit-grade architecture](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/closed-loop-ticketing/) — Reference for closed-loop fare-collection deployments: DESFire credentials, SAM-protected validators, offline transactions, anti-fraud surface, settlement.
- [Device Identity at Scale — hardware roots, lifecycle](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/device-identity-at-scale/) — Architecture for device identity at scale: hardware roots of trust, manufacturing-time provisioning, OTA lifecycle, V2X EA/AA, eSIM SM-DP+, fleet revocation.
- [eSIM Security — AmbiSecure | SGP.22 / SGP.32 / RSP](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/esim-security/) — eSIM security — eUICC OS / applets, SM-DP+ integration, GSMA SGP.22 (consumer) / SGP.32 (M2M) compliance, security review across the stack.
- [Government Identity — high-assurance, attested](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/government-identity/) — Government identity programmes with NIST AAL3 / FIPS 140-3 / CC EAL6+ / eIDAS coverage. Sovereign personalisation, sovereign attestation roots, air-gap-tolerant validation.
- [JavaCard Applet Deployment — AmbiSecure | Spec to shipment](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/javacard-deployment/) — JavaCard applet design, implementation, verification, personalisation, and field lifecycle. FIDO2, PIV, OpenPGP, EMV, eID, custom applets at scale.
- [Offline authentication](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/offline-authentication/) — Offline-authentication architecture — hardware-rooted credential, per-session crypto, deferred reconciliation. Pattern, principles, and where to apply it.
- [Passwordless & MFA — hardware-bound enterprise auth](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/passwordless-mfa/) — Replace passwords + OTP with hardware-bound FIDO2 credentials — phishing-resistant by construction. Reference architecture, migration path, deployment options.
- [Passwordless Enterprise — real, not hidden passwords](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/passwordless-enterprise/) — Real passwordless enterprise — what an end-to-end deployment really looks like. Authenticators, provisioning, recovery, federation, attestation policy, lifecycle automation.
- [Phishing-resistant Authentication — AmbiSecure | What it really means](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/phishing-resistant-authentication/) — Phishing-resistant MFA has a precise meaning — origin-bound, hardware-backed credentials. What WebAuthn buys, and how it maps to AAL3 / CISA / OMB M-22-09.
- [Secure Element Integration — AmbiSecure | Silicon-to-cloud](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/secure-element-integration/) — Secure-element integration into your product — chip selection, host driver, JavaCard applet, lifecycle. NXP, Infineon, STMicro families. CC EAL6+ secure elements.
- [Secure validator platforms](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/secure-validator-platforms/) — Validator hardware that survives tamper, environmental stress, and unattended deployment. SAM-protected, signed-firmware, field-serviceable.
- [Smart access control](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/smart-access-control/) — Physical access control with FIDO2 + DESFire-class credentials. One card opens doors, signs into laptops, badges into elevators.
- [Smart-Card Personalisation — AmbiSecure | Issuance line](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/smart-card-personalization/) — Smart-card personalisation as a service or in-country line. SCP03 + HSM-backed issuance for FIDO2 / PIV / EMV / eID applets. CC EAL6+ secure elements.
- [Solutions — passwordless, IoT root of trust, provisioning](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/) — AmbiSecure solutions: passwordless MFA, IoT root of trust, secure provisioning, digital identity, payment security, secure firmware update.
- [Transit ticketing — closed-loop deployments](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/transit-ticketing/) — Closed-loop transit-ticketing deployments — DESFire cards, SAM-protected validators, depot edge, back-office settlement.
- [V2X Security Architecture — OBU, RSU, and ITS PKI](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/v2x-security/) — Cryptographic trust architecture for V2X infrastructure — EA/AA PKI, pseudonymous certificates, hardware-isolated key storage, secure manufacturing.
- [Workforce Identity — AmbiSecure | Hardware-backed enterprise identity](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/workforce-identity/) — Workforce identity programme — issuance, daily authentication, recovery, off-boarding. Hardware-rooted with attestation-anchored AAGUID policy and IdP-layer federation.

## Technologies

- [Card ↔ Reader ↔ SAM — mutual-authentication architecture](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/card-reader-sam-flow/) — Reference walkthrough of the trust chain that powers closed-loop DESFire deployments — trust zones, key hierarchy, mutual auth flow, offline lifecycle, validator form factors.
- [CTAP2 — AmbiSecure | Client-To-Authenticator Protocol](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/ctap2/) — CTAP2 is the FIDO Client-To-Authenticator Protocol — wire format browsers use to talk to USB-HID, NFC, and BLE roaming authenticators. Commands, transports.
- [FIDO & WebAuthn — AmbiSecure | Phishing-resistant authentication](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/fido/) — How FIDO2 / WebAuthn / CTAP2 work — the trust chain, registration, authentication, attestation, AAGUIDs — and the AmbiSecure products and services that ship it.
- [FIDO2 — AmbiSecure | The alliance, the spec, the certification](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/fido2/) — FIDO2 is two specs working together: WebAuthn (W3C) and CTAP2 (FIDO Alliance). What each does, what is in the family, and how certification levels work.
- [JavaCard — AmbiSecure | Bytecode applets on Secure Elements](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/javacard/) — What JavaCard actually is — bytecode, lifecycle, CAP files, SCP03 loading, persistent vs transient memory — and where it fits in our products and services.
- [MIFARE DESFire — EV1, EV2, EV3 architecture](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/desfire/) — What changed across DESFire EV1, EV2, EV3 — application/file model, authentication, secure messaging, transaction MAC. Engineering reference, no exploit content.
- [Passkeys — BE/BS, syncable vs device-bound](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/passkeys/) — Passkeys are WebAuthn credentials with BE/BS posture. The engineering page — what to require, what to accept, what to reject in device-bound vs syncable.
- [Secure Access Modules — SAM AV2 / AV3 architecture](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/sam/) — Why closed-loop transit and access systems put issuer keys in a Secure Access Module, not in reader firmware. SAM AV2/AV3 capabilities, trust zones, host channel.
- [Secure elements](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/secure-elements/) — What a secure element is, how it differs from TPM and HSM, and where AmbiSecure uses them across cards, USB keys, IoT chipsets, and applet platforms.
- [Technologies — JavaCard, FIDO, Secure Elements, NFC, eSIM](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/) — The technologies AmbiSecure builds with: JavaCard, Secure Elements, FIDO, NFC/DESFire, PKI, eSIM, APDU, embedded cryptography.
- [V2X PKI — IEEE 1609.2 & ETSI TS 103 097](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/v2x-pki/) — Technical reference for V2X PKI: Enrolment Credentials, Pseudonymous Certificates, certificate lifecycle, and hardware binding requirements for connected vehicle deployments.
- [WebAuthn Attestation — fmt, x5c, MDS, AAGUID](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/attestation/) — WebAuthn attestation — fmt formats, attStmt structure, x5c chain validation, FIDO MDS BLOB integration, AAGUID allow-listing, enterprise attestation.
- [WebAuthn — registration, authentication, attestation](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/webauthn/) — WebAuthn (W3C) — registration and authentication ceremonies, clientDataJSON, authenticatorData, attestation objects, RP ID rules, BE/BS flags, with diagrams.

## Standards evolution timelines

- [ePassport evolution timeline — AmbiSecure resources](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/epassport/) — A chronological timeline of ePassport standards evolution: ICAO 9303, BAC, PACE, SAC, EAC, multi-application travel documents.
- [FIDO evolution timeline — AmbiSecure resources](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/fido/) — A chronological timeline of FIDO standards evolution, from FIDO 1.0 / U2F through CTAP, FIDO2, WebAuthn, and modern multi-device passkeys.
- [OTP and SMS authentication evolution timeline — AmbiSecure resources](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/otp-sms/) — A chronological timeline of one-time-password authentication standards: S/Key, RSA SecurID, HOTP, TOTP, SMS OTP, and the FIDO-driven phishing-resistance pivot.
- [PIV evolution timeline — AmbiSecure resources](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/piv/) — A chronological timeline of PIV standards evolution — FIPS 201, SP 800-73, derived PIV credentials, and the modern multi-form-factor PIV deployment.
- [Secure-element evolution timeline — AmbiSecure resources](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/secure-elements/) — A chronological timeline of secure-element evolution — from SIM cards through embedded SEs, TPMs, eUICCs, and IoT trust chips.
- [Smart-card evolution timeline — AmbiSecure resources](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/smart-cards/) — A chronological timeline of smart-card standards and platform evolution: ISO 7816, JavaCard, GlobalPlatform, DESFire, multi-applet smart cards.
- [Standards evolution timelines — AmbiSecure resources](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/) — Decade-scale evolution of the standards AmbiSecure builds against — FIDO, PIV, ePassport, OTP, smart cards, WebAuthn, secure elements.
- [WebAuthn and passkey evolution timeline — AmbiSecure resources](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/webauthn-passkey/) — A chronological timeline of WebAuthn and passkey evolution — W3C standardisation, browser support, and the platform shift to synced passkeys.

## Engineering references

- [APDU Status Words — ISO 7816, GP, EMV, FIDO](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/apdu-status/) — Searchable APDU status word (SW1/SW2) reference — ISO/IEC 7816-4 standard codes plus GlobalPlatform, EMV, and FIDO U2F-specific values.
- [ASN.1 Universal Tags Reference — AmbiSecure | ITU-T X.680](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/asn1/) — Searchable ASN.1 universal-class tag reference. BOOLEAN, INTEGER, OCTET STRING, OBJECT IDENTIFIER, SEQUENCE, SET, and the string types.
- [DESFire Command Set Reference — EV1/EV2/EV3](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/desfire-commands/) — Searchable MIFARE DESFire command reference — command name, code, and EV1/EV2/EV3 support, grouped by class. Native and ISO 7816-4 wrapped commands.
- [DESFire Status Codes Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/desfire/) — Searchable DESFire status code reference — every documented EV1/EV2/EV3 response status with meaning and context.
- [EMV Tags Reference — AmbiSecure | Books 3, 4, and Contactless](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/emv-tags/) — Searchable EMV tag reference covering Books 3, 4, and Contactless. Filter by category — card-data, transaction, crypto, capability, DOL, template.
- [Engineering References — smart-card, FIDO, V2X PKI](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/) — Engineering reference DB: APDU status words, EMV tags, ISO 7816, COSE algs, FIDO AAGUIDs, DESFire, GlobalPlatform, ASN.1, X.509, IEEE 1609.2, SGP.32.
- [ETSI TS 102 941 V2X Trust & Privacy Management Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/etsi-ts-102-941/) — ETSI TS 102 941 reference. V2X PKI hierarchy, Enrolment Authority, Authorisation Authority, EC/PC issuance flows, Butterfly Key Expansion, CRL and CTL distribution.
- [ETSI TS 103 097 V2X Secured-Message Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/etsi-ts-103-097/) — ETSI TS 103 097 reference — V2X SecuredMessage structure, header info fields, signer-info modes, profile constraints, and equivalence with IEEE 1609.2.
- [FIDO AAGUIDs Reference — AmbiSecure | Curated authenticator directory](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/aaguids/) — Searchable AAGUID directory for FIDO2 / WebAuthn authenticators. Curated subset of well-known consumer and enterprise devices.
- [GlobalPlatform Status Words Reference — AmbiSecure | GP 2.3.1](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/globalplatform/) — Searchable reference for GlobalPlatform 2.3.1 status words across card-management commands (INSTALL, LOAD, DELETE, GET STATUS).
- [GSMA SGP.32 IoT eSIM Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/sgp-32/) — GSMA SGP.32 IoT eSIM reference — eUICC architecture, IPA (IoT Profile Assistant), SM-DP+, SM-DS, IoT Profile Discovery, parallels to V2X EA / AA lifecycle.
- [IEEE 1609.2 V2X Certificate Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/ieee-1609-2/) — IEEE 1609.2 V2X cert reference — structure, COER encoding, IssuerIdentifier, ToBeSignedCertificate, validity, verification key, signatures, vs X.509.
- [ISO 21177 ITS Station Security Services Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/iso-21177/) — ISO 21177 reference — ITS station security service architecture, secure session establishment, and the bridge from 1609.2 / 102 941 PKI to session protocols.
- [ISO/IEC 7816 CLA Values Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/iso7816/) — Searchable reference for common ISO/IEC 7816-4 CLA byte values — interindustry, proprietary, GlobalPlatform, secure-messaging variants.
- [JavaCard CAP Components Reference — AmbiSecure | JCVM 3.x](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/javacard-cap/) — Reference for the 12 standard JavaCard CAP file components — Header, Directory, Applet, Import, ConstantPool, Class, Method, StaticField, and friends.
- [NFC NDEF TNF Types Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/nfc/) — Reference for NDEF Type Name Format (TNF) values — well-known, MIME, absolute URI, external types, plus the special and reserved values.
- [WebAuthn COSE Algorithms Reference — AmbiSecure | RFC 8152](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/webauthn-cose/) — Searchable reference for COSE algorithm identifiers used in WebAuthn / FIDO2. ES256, EdDSA, RS256, PS256, and the full IANA registry subset.
- [WebAuthn Reference — AmbiSecure | Developer documentation portal](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/webauthn/) — WebAuthn engineering reference — registration, authentication, attestation, RP ID rules, BE/BS flags, troubleshooting. Static, fast, copy-able code samples.
- [X.509 / Certificate OIDs Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/x509-oids/) — Searchable reference for common X.509 / PKIX OIDs — RDN attribute types, certificate extensions, signature algorithms, FIDO attestation extensions.
- [X.509 Extensions Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/x509-extensions/) — Reference for common X.509 v3 certificate extensions — SKID, AKID, KU, EKU, SAN, BC, CDP, AIA, certificatePolicies, FIDO AAGUID extension.

## Engineering tools

- [AAGUID Lookup — AmbiSecure | FIDO2 authenticator directory](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/aaguid-lookup/) — Look up FIDO2 / WebAuthn AAGUIDs against a curated offline directory of well-known consumer and enterprise authenticators.
- [APDU Builder — AmbiSecure | ISO 7816-4 command APDU constructor](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/apdu-builder/) — Build a valid ISO 7816-4 command APDU from CLA/INS/P1/P2/data/Le with live case detection and validation, or parse APDU hex back into fields. Client-side.
- [APDU Parser — ISO 7816-4 command & response](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/apdu-parser/) — Free, client-side APDU parser. Decode CLA / INS / P1 / P2 / Lc / Le for Case 1-4 (short and extended length), or response APDUs with status word lookup.
- [APDU Script Validator — AmbiSecure | ISO 7816-4 case 1-4](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/apdu-script-validator/) — Validate a multi-line APDU script line-by-line for ISO 7816-4 case 1, 2, 3, 4 — short and extended length. Validation only; no execution.
- [APDU Status Dictionary — AmbiSecure | Full SW1/SW2 reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/apdu-status-dict/) — Browseable, filterable dictionary of smart-card status words from ISO 7816-4, GlobalPlatform, EMV, and FIDO.
- [ASCII ↔ HEX Converter — AmbiSecure | Bidirectional ASCII / HEX](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/ascii-hex/) — Convert ASCII text to / from HEX. Multiple separator styles, copy-friendly. Client-side only.
- [ASN.1 BER/DER Parser — AmbiSecure | Walk any ASN.1 structure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/asn1-parser/) — Decode any BER/DER-encoded ASN.1 blob into a clickable tree, with X.509 OID lookup. Hex, base64, or PEM input. Client-side.
- [ASN.1 Tree Explorer — AmbiSecure | DER walker with collapsible nodes](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/asn1-tree-explorer/) — Walk DER-encoded ASN.1 as a collapsible tree with OID lookup, primitive type recognition, and per-node hex preview. PEM input accepted.
- [ATR Parser — decode ISO/IEC 7816-3 Answer-To-Reset](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/atr-parser/) — Free, client-side ATR parser. Decode TS, T0, TA/TB/TC/TD interface bytes, historical bytes, and TCK from any smart-card ATR. Nothing leaves your browser.
- [ATS Parser — AmbiSecure | Decode ISO/IEC 14443-4 Answer-To-Select](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/ats-parser/) — Decode ISO/IEC 14443-4 ATS bytes — TL, T0, TA(1)/TB(1)/TC(1), historical bytes. Client-side.
- [authenticatorData Parser — AmbiSecure | WebAuthn](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/authdata-parser/) — Walk WebAuthn authenticatorData — rpIdHash, flags, signCount, AAGUID, credentialId, COSE_Key, extensions.
- [Base32 Encoder and Decoder — AmbiSecure | RFC 4648 & base32hex](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/base32/) — Encode and decode Base32 and base32hex (RFC 4648) in your browser — optional padding, case-insensitive input, text or hex modes. Useful for TOTP / 2FA secrets.
- [Base64 Certificate Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/base64-cert/) — Detect and decode any certificate blob — PEM, base64, or hex — and emit DER bytes plus a clean PEM. Client-side, multi-block.
- [Base64 Encoder / Decoder — standard & URL-safe](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/base64/) — Encode or decode base64 (standard and URL-safe variants). Client-side, copy-friendly, sample inputs.
- [Base64URL Converter — AmbiSecure | RFC 4648 §5](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/base64url/) — Encode and decode base64url (RFC 4648 §5). The format WebAuthn, JOSE, and CTAP2 use everywhere. Bidirectional conversion — base64url, base64, hex, UTF-8.
- [BER Length Visualizer — AmbiSecure | ITU-T X.690](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/ber-length/) — Decode and encode BER length octets — short-form, long-form, and indefinite-form. Quick reference for ASN.1 and EMV TLV encoding.
- [Binary Calculator — AmbiSecure | bitwise ops & base converter](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/binary-calculator/) — Convert between binary, decimal, hex, and octal and run bitwise AND/OR/XOR/NOT and shifts at 8/16/32/64-bit width with signed/unsigned and two’s-complement views. Client-side.
- [Byte Offset Calculator — AmbiSecure | hex offset & field inspector](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/byte-offset-calculator/) — Paste hex bytes for an indexed offset table with decimal/hex offsets, grouping, ASCII preview, and little/big-endian integer interpretation. For APDU/TLV debugging.
- [CBOR Decoder — AmbiSecure | RFC 8949 / WebAuthn / CTAP2](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/cbor-decoder/) — Decode CBOR (RFC 8949) blobs in your browser — integers, byte / text strings, arrays, maps, tags. Friendly for WebAuthn attestation objects, COSE keys, CTAP2 messages.
- [Certificate Chain Viewer — AmbiSecure | Inspect a PEM bundle](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/cert-chain/) — Visualise a TLS / PKI certificate chain from a multi-cert PEM bundle. Each cert summarised; chain links validated by issuer ↔ subject. Client-side.
- [Certificate Fingerprint — SHA-1/256/512](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/cert-fingerprint/) — Compute SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512 fingerprints of an X.509 certificate. Uses Web Crypto. Fully client-side.
- [Checksum Calculator — AmbiSecure | sum, XOR, Fletcher, Adler-32](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/checksum/) — Compute 8/16/32-bit sums, one’s/two’s-complement, XOR, Fletcher-16/32, Adler-32, and the Internet (RFC 1071) checksum over text or hex — all client-side with endian options.
- [clientDataJSON Decoder — AmbiSecure | WebAuthn ceremony](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/clientdata-decoder/) — Decode a base64url clientDataJSON. Verifies type, extracts challenge / origin / crossOrigin, and computes SHA-256 — what the authenticator actually signs.
- [CMAC Length Calculator — AmbiSecure | NIST SP 800-38B](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/cmac-length/) — Compute CMAC block count, padded length, and output truncation for AES / TDES. Practical companion for SCP03, DESFire EV2/EV3, and EMV CDA implementers.
- [COSE Key Inspector — AmbiSecure | RFC 8152 / WebAuthn](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/cose-key/) — Inspect COSE_Key blobs (RFC 8152) — kty, alg, crv, x, y, n, e. Decode the credentialPublicKey from any WebAuthn attestation.
- [CRC Calculator — AmbiSecure | 70+ CRC presets & custom polynomials](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/crc-calculator/) — Compute CRCs with 70+ catalogue presets (CRC-8/16/32/64, CCITT, XMODEM, CRC-32C…) or a fully custom width/poly/init/refin/refout/xorout. Verifies the 0x check value. Client-side.
- [Credential ID Inspector — AmbiSecure | WebAuthn diagnostic](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/credential-id/) — Inspect a WebAuthn credentialId — length, entropy, AAGUID-shaped prefix, encoding cross-checks. Diagnostic only; credentialIds are opaque to the RP.
- [CSR Decoder — AmbiSecure | Decode PKCS#10 CSRs client-side](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/csr-decoder/) — Decode a PKCS#10 Certificate Signing Request — subject, public key, requested extensions, signature algorithm. Client-side only, no upload.
- [DESFire Access-Rights Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/desfire-access-rights/) — Decode the 16-bit DESFire access-rights word into Read / Write / Read+Write / ChangeAccessRights key references.
- [DESFire File Settings Parser](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/desfire-file-settings/) — Decode the DESFire GetFileSettings response — file type, comms settings, access rights word, per-type fields (Std / Backup / Value / Linear / Cyclic Record / TMAC).
- [DESFire Key Settings Interpreter](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/desfire-key-settings/) — Decode the 2-byte DESFire GetKeySettings response — keySettings byte (ChangeKey / config / master-key flags), numKeys, and crypto type (DES / 3DES / AES).
- [DESFire Status Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/desfire-status/) — Look up DESFire response status bytes — OPERATION_OK, AUTHENTICATION_ERROR, ADDITIONAL_FRAME, etc. Client-side reference.
- [ECC Curve Reference — AmbiSecure | P-256, secp256k1, Curve25519](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/ecc-curve-reference/) — Elliptic curve reference: P-192/224/256/384/521, secp256k1, Brainpool, Ed25519/Ed448, X25519/X448 — field sizes, uses, and Web Crypto support, with demo keys.
- [EMV Tag Dictionary — AmbiSecure | filterable directory](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/emv-tag-dict/) — Filterable directory of EMV (Books 3, 4, and CL) tags — SELECT, GET PROCESSING OPTIONS, READ RECORD, GENERATE AC, terminal capabilities.
- [Endian Converter — AmbiSecure | Swap u16 / u32 / u64 byte order](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/endian/) — Convert between big-endian (BE) and little-endian (LE) for u16 / u32 / u64 hex blocks. Shows unsigned numeric value too.
- [eUICC EID Decoder — AmbiSecure | eSIM EID structure & checksum](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/euicc-eid-decoder/) — Decode a 32-digit eUICC EID into its issuer/version fields per GSMA SGP.02 / ITU-T E.118 and validate the trailing Luhn check digits — client-side, dummy examples only.
- [FIDO Metadata Explorer — AmbiSecure | AAGUID directory](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/fido-mds-explorer/) — Browse the AAGUID directory in metadata-style cards — vendor, model, transports, form factor and documented credential class. Searchable. Production should fetch the FIDO MDS BLOB.
- [GlobalPlatform Status Reference — AmbiSecure | Card & app lifecycle](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/gp-status/) — Reference for GlobalPlatform 2.3.1 — card lifecycle states, application lifecycle states, ISD permissions.
- [HEX ↔ Byte-Array Converter](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/hex-bytes/) — Convert hex strings into C / Java / Python / JS byte arrays — and back. Multiple programmer formats. Client-side only.
- [ICCID Decoder — AmbiSecure | SIM card number & Luhn validator](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/iccid-decoder/) — Decode an ICCID into MII, country code, issuer identifier, and account number, and validate the Luhn check digit — 19/20-digit aware, whitespace-tolerant. Uses dummy examples only.
- [IEEE 1609.2 V2X Certificate Parser](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/ieee-1609-2-parser/) — Client-side IEEE 1609.2 cert parser for V2X / ITS PKI. Decodes hex, Base64, and PEM-wrapped certificates into a field tree with standards references.
- [IMSI Decoder — AmbiSecure | MCC / MNC / MSIN breakdown](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/imsi-decoder/) — Decode an IMSI into MCC, MNC, and MSIN with 2- or 3-digit MNC handling and a small built-in MCC name map. Privacy-aware, dummy examples only, fully client-side.
- [ISO 7816-4 CLA Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/iso7816-cla/) — Decode the ISO/IEC 7816-4 CLA byte — interindustry vs proprietary, secure messaging bits, command chaining, logical channel.
- [ISO/IEC 14443 Frame Explorer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/iso14443-frame/) — Decode ISO/IEC 14443-3 (REQA / WUPA / ATQA / SAK) and 14443-4 (RATS / I-block / R-block / S-block) frame structures.
- [ISO/IEC 14443 Quick Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/iso14443-ref/) — ISO/IEC 14443 contactless smart cards: parts, Type A vs B, anti-collision, framing, I/R/S blocks. One-page reference.
- [ISO/IEC 7816 Quick Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/iso7816-ref/) — A bookmark-friendly quick reference card for ISO/IEC 7816 — APDU structure, common INS bytes, file types, security messaging.
- [JavaCard CAP Structure Explorer — AmbiSecure | JCVM 3.x](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/javacard-cap/) — Explore JavaCard CAP file components (Header, Directory, Applet, Import, ConstantPool, Class, Method, StaticField, RefLocation, Export, Descriptor, Debug).
- [JSON Bin Builder and Validator](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/json-bin-builder/) — Validate, format, minify, and save local JSON bins in your browser for API testing, static-site data, demos, and configuration examples.
- [JSON Formatter and Minifier — AmbiSecure | pretty-print JSON](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/json-formatter/) — Pretty-print, minify, and sort-key JSON in your browser with 2/4/tab indentation and precise error location. Client-side, copy and download. Pairs with the JSON validator.
- [JSON Validator — AmbiSecure | syntax & JSON Schema checker](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/json-validator/) — Validate JSON syntax with exact error location, and optionally check an instance against a JSON Schema — all client-side. No upload. Pairs with the JSON formatter.
- [Key Diversification Visualizer — AmbiSecure (Educational)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/key-diversification/) — Educational visualizer for AES-CMAC key diversification (NXP AN10922 / AV2). Shows the SHAPE of the derivation, not a production-grade computation.
- [Length-Field Encoder — AmbiSecure | BER, DGI, u8, u16 BE/LE](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/length-field/) — Encode an integer length in BER long-form, DGI long-form, 1-byte, and 2-byte BE/LE. Useful for APDU and TLV building.
- [LRC Calculator — AmbiSecure | longitudinal redundancy check](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/lrc-calculator/) — Compute LRC variants — XOR parity, 8/16-bit additive two’s-complement and modulo sums — over text or hex, with optional STX/ETX framing and a step-by-step table. Client-side.
- [NDEF Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/ndef-decoder/) — Decode NFC Data Exchange Format (NDEF) messages — URI, Text, Smart Poster, MIME, External records.
- [Passkey Flow Visualizer — AmbiSecure | Registration & authentication](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/passkey-flow-viz/) — Step-by-step walkthrough of the WebAuthn registration and authentication ceremonies, with annotation linking to the relevant decoder tools.
- [PEM ↔ DER Converter — client-side](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/pem-der/) — Free, client-side PEM ↔ DER converter for certificates, CSRs, public/private keys. PEM block detection, multi-block support, downloadable output.
- [PFX / PKCS#12 Inspector — read .pfx client-side](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/pfx-inspector/) — Inspect the structure of a .pfx / .p12 PKCS#12 archive — bag types, encryption schemes, embedded certificates. Client-side; password-decryption is a TODO.
- [RP ID / Origin Validator — AmbiSecure | WebAuthn §5.1.3](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/rp-id-validator/) — Validate WebAuthn rpId / origin combinations. Confirms registrable-suffix rule, HTTPS posture, public-suffix-list constraints.
- [RSA Key Formats — AmbiSecure | PKCS#1 vs PKCS#8 vs SPKI reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/rsa-key-formats/) — Reference and demo tool for RSA key formats: PKCS#1, PKCS#8, SubjectPublicKeyInfo, PEM vs DER, and JWK. Generate a demo key pair (2048/3072/4096) in-browser via Web Crypto. Client-side.
- [SCP03 Helper — AmbiSecure | session keys & cryptogram calculator](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/scp03-helper/) — Educational GlobalPlatform SCP03 helper: derive S-ENC/S-MAC/S-RMAC session keys and host/card cryptograms from static keys and challenges using AES-CMAC in your browser. Developer aid.
- [SCP03 Session Walkthrough — AmbiSecure (Educational)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/scp03-walkthrough/) — Educational walkthrough of GlobalPlatform SCP03 (AES-128 mutual authentication and secure messaging). No production key compute — illustration only.
- [Secure Messaging Field Visualizer — AmbiSecure | ISO 7816-4 §6](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/secure-messaging-viz/) — Walk SM-wrapped APDU body data — DOs 87 (cryptogram), 97 (Le), 99 (status), 8E (MAC), 84 (legacy cryptogram). Reference for ISO 7816-4 secure-messaging implementers.
- [Sequence Diagram Generator — auth, V2X, eSIM flows](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/sequence-diagram-generator/) — Client-side sequence diagram generator for security engineers. WebSequenceDiagrams syntax in, clean SVG/PNG out. Templates for FIDO2, SCP03, eSIM SGP.32, V2X PKI.
- [SHA Hash Generator — AmbiSecure | SHA-1/256/384/512, hex & Base64](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/sha-hash-generator/) — Generate SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512 digests of text, hex, or a local file using the browser’s Web Crypto API — hex and Base64 output, copy buttons. Nothing is uploaded.
- [SW1/SW2 Lookup — AmbiSecure | Search smart-card status words](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/sw-lookup/) — Look up smart-card status words (SW1 SW2) by hex or by phrase. ISO 7816-4, GlobalPlatform, EMV, FIDO common returns.
- [TLV Parser — AmbiSecure | Decode BER-TLV with EMV tag dictionary](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/tlv-parser/) — Free, client-side BER-TLV parser with an EMV tag dictionary. Walks tag/length/value trees of any depth. Nothing leaves your browser.
- [TLV Tree Visualizer — AmbiSecure | EMV / BER-TLV](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/tlv-tree-viz/) — Visualise BER-TLV / EMV TLV trees as collapsible nested nodes with EMV tag-name lookup. The visual companion to the TLV parser.
- [Transaction MAC Field Visualizer — AmbiSecure | DESFire EV2/EV3](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/tmac-visualizer/) — Visualise the DESFire EV2/EV3 Transaction MAC field — 4-byte counter (TMC) + 8-byte MAC value (TMV). Educational reference for transit / closed-loop ticketing implementers.
- [UID Analyzer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/uid-analyzer/) — Inspect ISO/IEC 14443 Type A UID — cascade level, manufacturer, fixed vs random.
- [URL Encoder and Decoder — AmbiSecure | percent-encoding tool](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/url-encoder/) — Percent-encode and decode URLs and URL components in your browser. Toggle space as %20 or +, encode full URLs vs components, handle malformed input. Client-side.
- [UTF-8 Inspector — AmbiSecure | Show codepoints & bytes per character](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/utf8/) — See exactly how UTF-8 encodes a string — codepoints, byte counts, BOM detection, per-character breakdown.
- [V2X Certificate Chain Validator](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/v2x-cert-chain-validator/) — Client-side structural validator for IEEE 1609.2 V2X cert chains. Checks parse, issuer linkage by HashedId8, validity, signature schemes.
- [WebAuthn Attestation Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/attestation-decoder/) — Decode WebAuthn attestationObject (CBOR with fmt, attStmt, authData). Walks attStmt and links into the authenticatorData parser.
- [WebAuthn Challenge Viewer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/challenge-viewer/) — Inspect a WebAuthn challenge — length, Shannon entropy, common pitfalls. Generate fresh 32-byte challenges with the platform CSPRNG.
- [X.509 Certificate Viewer — decode certs in-browser](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/x509-viewer/) — Decode an X.509 certificate (or chain) into subject, issuer, validity, public key, signature algorithm, and all extensions — fully client-side, no upload.
- [XML Formatter and Validator — AmbiSecure | pretty-print XML](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/xml-formatter/) — Format, minify, and well-formedness-check XML in your browser using the native DOMParser. Preserves attributes and text; reports parse errors. Client-side, no DTD processing.

## Engineering blog (modern)

- [AmbiSecure Products & CRA Readiness](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/ambisecure-products-cra-readiness/) — A product-security mapping of AmbiSecure offerings to CRA-aligned needs: AmbiSEC, ONE Pass, BioKey, FIDO and secure-element credentials for connected products.
- [APDU from First Principles — CLA, INS, P1/P2, SW](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/apdu-from-first-principles/) — A practical, deployment-focused walkthrough of ISO/IEC 7816-4 APDUs — Case 1-4, short and extended length, CLA bits, status words, and the gotchas. With code that compiles.
- [Building Secure IoT Identity with Security Applets](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/secure-iot-identity-with-applets/) — Five JavaCard applets for IoT device identity: provisioning, attestation, mTLS, signed firmware, key rotation. Architecture on a CC EAL6+ secure element.
- [CRA Secure by Design: Hardware-Backed Trust](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/cra-secure-by-design-hardware-backed-trust/) — Secure by design under the CRA: why hardware-backed trust, secure key storage, and device identity matter for connected products — with AmbiSEC as the trust layer.
- [CRA Vulnerability Handling & Lifecycle](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/cra-vulnerability-handling-product-lifecycle-security/) — CRA vulnerability handling and lifecycle security: reporting from 11 Sep 2026, support periods, disclosure, updates, and the evidence manufacturers must prepare.
- [Cyber Security Threats — What Actually Matters in 2026](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/cyber-security-threats-overview/) — A modern, clear-eyed overview of the cyber-security threats that drive identity and hardware-credential decisions in 2026 — phishing, MFA bypass, SIM swap.
- [DESFire EV1 vs EV2 vs EV3 — Architectural Evolution](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/desfire-ev1-vs-ev2-vs-ev3/) — What actually changed across DESFire EV1, EV2, and EV3 — secure messaging, transaction MAC, key diversification, side-channel hardening. Architecture-level pillar post.
- [Designing Enterprise Passwordless Systems — the deployment runbook](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/designing-enterprise-passwordless-systems/) — What a passwordless rollout actually looks like at workforce scale — nine load-bearing components, recovery flow, attestation policy, lifecycle, migration.
- [Designing Low-Latency Secure Transit Validators](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/designing-low-latency-secure-transit-validators/) — How to keep tap-to-decision under 300 ms while running EV2 mutual auth, transaction MAC, and audit writes. Engineering disciplines that decide whether the deployment ships.
- [Designing Secure Credential Lifecycle Management](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/credential-lifecycle-management/) — Credentials don't just get issued and used — they get re-issued, rotated, recovered, and revoked. The lifecycle every hardware-credential programme has to design.
- [Designing Secure Email and Document Signing Platforms](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/designing-email-document-signing-platforms/) — Architecture for S/MIME email signing, PDF/PKCS#7 document signing, hardware-backed credentials, Long-Term Validation, and the trust-list problem.
- [Device Identity at Manufacturing Scale](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/device-identity-at-manufacturing-scale/) — Per-device keys on the personalisation line, HSM-backed SCP03 custody, OTA rotation, revocation across tiers — V2X / eSIM / IoT convergence in engineering depth.
- [Embedded Secure-Element FIDO2 Authenticators for Enterprise Identity](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/sim-based-fido2-authenticators/) — How nano-card and MFF2 secure elements act as roaming or embedded FIDO2 authenticators — threat-model gains and enterprise deployment economics.
- [Engineering Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/) — Practical, code-first writing on FIDO, WebAuthn, JavaCard, DESFire, SAM-backed transit, passwordless rollouts, and smart-card engineering.
- [Engineering blog — page 2](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/page/2/) — Older posts from the AmbiSecure engineering blog. Pagination scaffold; modern blog currently fits on page 1.
- [Engineering ePassport Issuance and Identity Platforms](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/engineering-epassport-issuance-platforms/) — Architecture of an ICAO 9303 ePassport platform: CSCA/DSC PKI, LDS generation, SOd signing, enrolment frontend, personalisation backbone, inspection-system reference.
- [EU Cyber Resilience Act for IoT & Hardware](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/eu-cyber-resilience-act-connected-hardware-iot/) — What the EU Cyber Resilience Act means for connected hardware and IoT makers: products with digital elements, secure-by-design, lifecycle, and key deadlines.
- [How FIDO Authentication Works](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/how-fido-authentication-works/) — A clear, non-academic explanation of how FIDO2 / WebAuthn actually works — registration, authentication, origin binding, what device and server do.
- [How V2X PKI Works — EA, AA, pseudonymous certs](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/how-v2x-pki-works/) — V2X PKI in engineering depth: Root CA, EA, AA, EC, PC, HashedId8, Butterfly Key Expansion, CRL/CTL. IEEE 1609.2 + ETSI TS 102 941 in practice.
- [Implementing FIDO2 — a developer guide](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/implementing-fido2-developer-guide/) — A practical, deployment-focused walkthrough of FIDO2 — CTAP2, WebAuthn, attestation, registration, authentication. Code that compiles, choices that scale.
- [JavaCard Applet Development for Enterprise Identity](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/javacard-applet-development-enterprise-identity/) — Building JavaCard applets that ship: AID design, lifecycle, secure messaging, personalisation, and the mistakes to avoid in enterprise identity.
- [Lava Lamps and Cryptographic Entropy](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/lava-lamps-and-cryptographic-entropy/) — How Cloudflare's lava-lamp Wall of Entropy feeds randomness pools — and why hardware TRNGs in secure-element silicon underpin TLS, FIDO, V2X, and eSIM.
- [Passkeys vs Traditional MFA — what really changed](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/passkeys-vs-traditional-mfa/) — Passkeys are not MFA-just-stronger. They are a different cryptographic primitive that retires phishing, replay, credential stuffing, and push fatigue as attack classes.
- [PIV Smart Cards vs USB Tokens vs Embedded Secure Elements](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/piv-vs-usb-tokens-vs-embedded/) — Workforce identity matrix: PIV smart card vs USB token vs embedded secure element. Lifecycle, physical-logical convergence, certificate workflows.
- [PKI Credential Issuance — workforce, government](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/pki-credential-issuance-workforce-government/) — Issuing X.509 credentials at workforce or government scale is more architecture than crypto — RA, CA, key custody, attestation, lifecycle, audit.
- [Platform vs Roaming Authenticators — picking the right form factor](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/platform-vs-roaming-authenticators/) — Platform and roaming FIDO2 authenticators look interchangeable on a slide and behave differently in production. The decision matrix and the right hybrid mix.
- [Pseudonymous Certificates and Privacy in V2X](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/pseudonymous-certificates-and-privacy/) — The privacy property of V2X PKI in depth: unlinkability, Butterfly Key Expansion, EA/AA split, and revocation that does not de-anonymise the fleet.
- [Secure Element vs TPM vs HSM — Where Each Fits](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/secure-element-vs-tpm-vs-hsm/) — Three classes of hardware key-storage that everyone confuses. What each is actually for, what each refuses to do, and how to choose for device, workstation, or backend.
- [Secure Elements in Connected Vehicles](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/secure-elements-in-connected-vehicles/) — The secure element in connected vehicles: key isolation, hardware-bound identity, anti-cloning, secure boot, OTA trust anchors. SIM, MFF2, TPM vs HSM vs SE.
- [Smart Cards vs FIDO Tokens vs Passkeys](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/smart-cards-vs-fido-tokens-vs-passkeys/) — A decision-grade comparison of smart-card authenticators, dedicated FIDO keys, and passkeys — threat model, lifecycle, recovery, choosing per deployment.
- [Top 3 Benefits of Multi-factor Authentication](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/top-3-benefits-of-mfa/) — The three reasons MFA isn't optional any more — and what changes when the second factor is rooted in hardware rather than an OTP seed in someone's phone.
- [Understanding WebAuthn Attestation Objects](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/understanding-webauthn-attestation-objects/) — WebAuthn attestation as engineering, not spec text — the seven formats, the verification pipeline, MDS BLOB, AAGUID allow-lists, production pitfalls.
- [Where Your AmbiSecure FIDO Key Works](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/fido-supported-services/) — A current, vendor-neutral overview of the services and platforms that accept FIDO2 / WebAuthn authentication — cloud SaaS, OS-level sign-in, IdPs, and developer platforms.
- [Why Hardware-Backed Identity Matters — the cost-per-extraction frame](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/why-hardware-backed-identity-matters/) — Hardware moves the cost-per-extraction of a credential up by orders of magnitude. The threat surfaces software cannot defend, and what hardware changes.
- [Why SAMs Matter in Closed-Loop Transit Systems](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/why-sams-matter-in-closed-loop-transit/) — Why every closed-loop transit deployment over a few thousand cards needs a Secure Access Module. Containment, diversification, auditable issuance, host channel.
- [Why Software-Only Device Trust Fails](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/why-software-only-device-trust-fails/) — Why field-deployed hardware cannot rely on software identity: firmware extraction, key cloning, replay, fleet-scale compromise. Software vs TPM vs secure element.
- [Why Transit Validators Need Offline Trust Architecture](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/transit-validators-offline-trust-architecture/) — Closed-loop transit ticketing has to keep collecting fares when the backend is unreachable. The architecture that lets validators do it without phoning home.
- [Why use Multi-factor Authentication?](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/why-use-multi-factor-authentication/) — Cybercriminals have billions of leaked credentials. A second factor — especially a hardware-bound one — cuts off the long tail of credential-stuffing and phishing.

## Engineering archive (2017–2025)

- [Challenges to IoT Security (Part 1) (Archive 2020) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/iot-security-challenges-part-1/) — Part one of a two-part look at IoT security — how billions of devices talk to enterprise systems, the attack surface that follows, and the trust anchors that contain it.
- [Challenges to IoT Security (Part 2) (Archive 2020) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/iot-security-challenges-part-2/) — Part two of a 2020 two-part look at IoT security — further challenges: infrequent firmware updates, small-scale attacks, autonomous systems and user privacy.
- [Common Misconceptions About 2FA | AmbiSecure (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/common-misconceptions-about-2fa/) — Unpacks common misconceptions about 2FA — what it actually defends against, where SMS codes fall short, why hardware-rooted second factors hold up.
- [Consumer Biometrics & Privacy | AmbiSecure (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/consumer-biometrics-and-privacy/) — Deploying biometric auth while meeting modern privacy expectations — match-on-device vs match-on-server, template storage, and the regulatory shape.
- [Cyber Attacks in India (Archive 2021) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/cyber-attacks-in-india-part-1/) — Part one of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India and what they reveal about systemic gaps in online identity, authentication, and incident response.
- [Cyber Attacks in India – Part 2 (Archive 2021) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/cyber-attacks-in-india-part-2/) — Part two of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India — attack patterns, identity weaknesses they exploited, and the auth posture that would have helped.
- [Cyber Attacks in India – Part 3 (Archive 2021) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/cyber-attacks-in-india-part-3/) — Part three of a three-part analysis of cyber attacks in India — closing on systemic gaps in incident response, identity hygiene, hardware-rooted adoption.
- [EMV Certification in Public Transport | AmbiSecure (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/emv-certification-in-public-transport/) — A practical look at EMV certification in public transport — which certifications apply, roles of acquirer / scheme / vendor / authority, typical timeline.
- [Engineering Archive — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/) — AmbiSecure engineering archive: FIDO, MFA, JavaCard, smart cards, transit, IoT security pieces from 2017 onwards, with links forward to current coverage.
- [Enterprise Security Threats (Archive 2021) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/enterprise-security-threats/) — A field overview of enterprise security threats in the cloud era — how the attack surface expands with new platforms, and where hardware identity narrows it.
- [ePassport and Its Application (Archive 2021) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/epassport-applications/) — How biometric passports work in practice — chip-based identity, border-control architecture, and the standards stack that keeps the issuer chain trustworthy.
- [Fast Identity Online (FIDO) (Archive 2020) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/fast-identity-online/) — A primer on FIDO and the Universal Second Factor model — how FIDO replaces shared secrets with hardware-bound credentials, and why phishing resistance changes.
- [How Chip-Based ePassports Work | AmbiSecure (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/how-chip-based-epassports-work/) — How chip-based ePassports work in practice — the BAC / PACE access protocols, the CSCA / DSC / PKD trust chain, and the role of the secure element inside the document.
- [Introduction to Java Card | AmbiSecure (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/introduction-to-java-card/) — An introduction to JavaCard — how applets run securely on smart cards and small-memory secure elements, and what the runtime enables for FIDO, PIV, ePassport.
- [Is Passwordless the future? (Archive 2021) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/is-passwordless-the-future/) — Examines whether passwordless authentication can scale beyond pilots — the standards, attack-surface trade-offs, and operational realities that decide its trajectory.
- [MFA in Government Sector | AmbiSecure (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/mfa-in-government/) — Why government identity programmes are adopting MFA, how MFA holds up against phishing and credential theft, and where hardware-rooted authenticators sit.
- [Public Transport Ticketing (Part 1) | AmbiSecure (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/public-transport-ticketing-part-1/) — First of three articles on automated fare collection — a comparative look at ticketing architectures across advanced economies and large transit networks in Asia.
- [Public Transport Ticketing (Part 2) | AmbiSecure (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/public-transport-ticketing-part-2/) — Part two of a 2020 survey of public transport ticketing — how fare collection worked in the United Kingdom, Germany, Russia, Brazil and Mexico.
- [Public Transport Ticketing (Part 3) | AmbiSecure (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/public-transport-ticketing-part-3/) — Part three of a 2020 survey of public transport ticketing — how fare collection worked in Singapore, Indonesia and Hong Kong.
- [Securing your IIoT infrastructure (Archive 2020) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/securing-iiot-infrastructure/) — Why industrial IoT remains a high-value target — markets driving adoption, trust gaps in legacy OT, and how hardware-rooted device identity tightens them.
- [Single Sign-On vs MFA | AmbiSecure (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/single-sign-on-vs-mfa/) — Compares single sign-on with multi-factor authentication — when each model fits, where they overlap, and how SSO + MFA combine for enterprise identity.
- [SMS-Based OTP Disadvantages | AmbiSecure (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/sms-otp-disadvantages/) — Why SMS-based one-time passwords are no longer a defensible second factor — SIM-swap attacks, SS7 weaknesses, phishing-prone OTP relay, and the hardware-rooted alternatives.
- [What is Passwordless Authentication? (Archive 2021) — AmbiSecure Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/what-is-passwordless-authentication/) — A primer on passwordless authentication — what it actually means, how FIDO and biometrics replace shared secrets, why hardware-bound credentials sit at the centre.
- [Workplace Biometrics — the New Normal | AmbiSecure (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/workplace-biometrics/) — How workplace biometrics layer onto existing auth — face / fingerprint / palm, privacy controls that keep them defensible, where they sit beside FIDO and MFA.

## Case studies

- [Case studies](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/case-studies/) — Anonymised architecture write-ups from real AmbiSecure deployments — passwordless workforce, transit ticketing, secure device identity.
- [Closed-loop transit ticketing — AmbiSecure case study](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/case-studies/transit-ticketing/) — DESFire EV2 + SAM-backed validator architecture for a metro operator's 700+ gate closed-loop ticketing fleet.
- [Passwordless workforce authentication — AmbiSecure case study](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/case-studies/passwordless-workforce/) — How a 9,000-seat workforce migrated from SMS and TOTP to FIDO2 across Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, and a legacy CAS portal.
- [Secure device identity for IoT — AmbiSecure case study](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/case-studies/secure-device-identity/) — Adding hardware-rooted device identity to a connected-device line: secure element on the BOM, SMT-line personalisation, and PKI lifecycle.

## Industries

- [Connected Mobility & V2X Security](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/connected-mobility/) — Hardware-backed identity for V2X infrastructure — SE integration for OBUs and RSUs, V2X PKI architecture, and ITS cert lifecycle for India's connected road ecosystem.
- [Enterprise access — AmbiSecure | One credential, every door](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/enterprise-access/) — Combined physical access + passwordless authentication using a single FIDO2 + DESFire-class card. Pilot to deployment in months.
- [Government & Defence — PIV, eID, civil-servant auth](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/government-and-defence/) — PIV-style smart cards, eID, digital signature tokens, custom JavaCard applets for government and defence deployments. Audit-grade issuance.
- [Industries — BFSI, Government, Telecom, IoT, Transit](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/) — Where AmbiSecure ships: banking and payments, government and defence, telecom, healthcare, IoT and industrial, retail and hospitality.
- [Smart cities — multi-application credentials at scale](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/smart-cities/) — Multi-application DESFire credentials for transit + city services. SAM-based key custody that survives an inter-departmental procurement model.
- [Transportation — Transit ticketing & validator platforms](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/transportation/) — Closed-loop fare collection, secure validator hardware, SAM-protected key custody for metros, BRT, light rail, ferries, and bus fleets.

## Videos

- [AmbiSecure BioKey — product hero loop — AmbiSecure Videos](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/biokey-product-loop/) — A short product hero loop showing the AmbiSecure BioKey biometric USB security key.
- [AmbiSecure card — all use cases — AmbiSecure Videos](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/ambisecure-card-use-cases/) — A 1m 27s overview of the AmbiSecure smart card across FIDO, PIV, door access, NDEF, and OpenPGP roles — what the multi-application card can carry.
- [AmbiSecure Videos — FIDO setup walkthroughs and product demos](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/) — AmbiSecure tutorial videos: FIDO U2F setup on Gmail, Facebook, mobile; AmbiSecure card use-case overview; BioKey product loop.
- [Set up AmbiSecure card on desktop (U2F/FIDO) | Videos](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/setup-ambisecure-card-desktop/) — A 1m 15s walkthrough of registering the AmbiSecure smart card as a FIDO U2F authenticator on a desktop browser.
- [Set up AmbiSecure card on Facebook (U2F/FIDO) | Videos](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/setup-ambisecure-card-facebook/) — Pair a hardware FIDO key with a Facebook account in under 90 seconds. Covers the security key tab under settings, registration, and verification.
- [Set up AmbiSecure card on Gmail (U2F/FIDO) | Videos](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/setup-ambisecure-card-gmail/) — Pair the AmbiSecure card with a Google account. Walkthrough of 2-step verification → security key registration on a desktop browser.
- [Set up card on phone — Facebook | AmbiSecure Videos](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/setup-ambisecure-card-mobile-facebook/) — Quick 24-second clip showing the NFC tap-and-register flow for the AmbiSecure card with the Facebook mobile app.
- [Set up card on phone — Gmail | AmbiSecure Videos](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/setup-ambisecure-card-mobile-gmail/) — A 1m 15s walkthrough of NFC-tap registration of the AmbiSecure card with a Google account from a mobile device.

## Brochures

- [Brochures](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/) — Downloadable / printable overviews of each AmbiSecure platform — OnePass, FIDO, transit security, JavaCard, PKI, device identity.
- [Device identity / IoT brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/device-identity/) — Hardware-rooted device identity for fleet-class IoT — secure element on the BOM, SMT-line personalisation, EST-based rotation.
- [FIDO deployment brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/fido/) — What an enterprise FIDO2 rollout actually looks like — AAGUID policy, attestation, recovery, common deployment mistakes.
- [JavaCard platform brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/javacard/) — Multi-applet JavaCard platform — FIDO2, PIV, OpenPGP, NDEF, biometric variants, IoT applets, custom enterprise applets, AID-selectable.
- [OnePass platform brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/onepass/) — OnePass platform overview — smart card, USB key, bio card, validation server, lifecycle. Engineered for hardware-backed identity.
- [PKI & credential lifecycle brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/pki/) — Workforce and government-scale PKI — RA, CA, key custody, attestation, lifecycle, audit. Designed to be defensible.
- [Transit security brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/transit-security/) — Closed-loop transit ticketing with DESFire EV2/EV3, SAM-backed validators, offline trust, and sub-300 ms tap-and-go.

## Engagement models

- [Engagement models](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/engagement-models/) — How AmbiSecure engagements are scoped — from architecture reviews to multi-quarter rollouts. No published price list; engagements are scoped to the problem.

## About

- [About AmbiSecure — hardware-rooted security](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/about/) — AmbiSecure is the security business unit of Ambimat Electronics. Forty years of embedded systems experience, focused on FIDO, JavaCard, Secure Element, and IoT trust.
- [Certifications & Standards — AmbiSecure | Trust documents](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/about/certifications/) — AmbiSecure certifications, conformance, and trust posture — FIDO, JavaCard 3.x, GlobalPlatform 2.3.1, ISO 7816, ISO 14443, NIST SP 800-73, plus disclaimers.

## Resources

- [AmbiSecure deck — hardware-rooted trust platform (slideshow)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/ambisecure-deck/) — A 20-slide overview of the AmbiSecure hardware-rooted trust platform: FIDO authenticators, secure-element engineering, Java Card services, PKI, IoT identity and engagement model.
- [Engineering Resources — parsers, references, tools](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/) — A toolbox of free, client-side utilities for smart-card, secure-element, and telecom engineers. ATR, APDU, TLV, ASN.1, CBOR, ICCID, NDEF, SCP03 — all in your browser.

## Contact

- [Contact AmbiSecure — Talk to engineers, not BDRs](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/contact/) — Get in touch with AmbiSecure. India and US sales lines, engineering inquiries, partnership requests. We respond from Ahmedabad, Gujarat.

- [Support — AmbiSecure | FAQs, guides, datasheets](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/support/) — Support hub for AmbiSecure customers — FAQs, integration guides, datasheets, and direct lines into engineering.

## Partners

- [Partners — AmbiSecure | Resellers, OEMs, system integrators, MSSPs](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/partners/) — AmbiSecure partner programme: resellers, OEMs, system integrators, MSSPs, and FIDO validation server white-label partners. Hardware-rooted security with co-branded options.

## Trust

- [Trust Center — certifications, posture, disclosures](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/trust/) — AmbiSecure Trust Center: certification posture, standards conformance, security model, disclosure practice, and trust documents on request.

## Privacy

- [Privacy & analytics preferences](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/privacy/) — Opt-out of analytics, review what AmbiSecure collects, and adjust your in-browser preference. DNT respected by default.

## Other

- [AmbiSecure FAQs — FIDO, PIV, JavaCard, secure elements](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/faqs/) — Answers to the questions engineering teams ask about AmbiSecure hardware-rooted identity — FIDO2 security keys, PIV smart cards, JavaCard applets, secure elements, eSIM authentication, PKI infrastructure, and…
- [AmbiSecure — Hardware-rooted identity: FIDO, JavaCard, V2X PKI](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/) — AmbiSecure builds hardware-rooted identity, FIDO authenticators, JavaCard applets, and IoT trust systems. Engineering-led, deployment-focused, ecosystem-aware.
- [Search AmbiSecure — products, services, technologies, blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/search/) — Sitewide client-side search across products, services, technologies, solutions, industries, case studies, references, blog, and archive. No backend.
